notes: M7 (scene persistence + authoring layer) IN PROGRESS — 5/9, active /goal 'Implement Milestone M7'. Gate-green (408 files / 2273 tests) @ 78434a6d. Implement M7 in task-number order T1..T9. M7-T5 DONE (78434a6d): prefab register + instantiate on the M7-T3/T4 codec. NEW packages/simulation/src/serialization/prefab.ts: instantiatePrefab(world, document, {registry, transform?, overrides?}) DEEP-CLONES the ApertureSceneDocument-shaped subtree (so two instances never alias each other's data), instantiates via loadScene (component registration + oldId->newEntity remap + Children rebuild + resolveWorldTransforms), returns the subtree ROOT (the record without a Parent), applies a per-field root LocalTransform override + per-prefab-id component-field overrides that fail loudly with a diagnostic on an unknown id/component/field. NEW componentRegistryFromWorld(world) (public getComponentByTypeId scan) so same-world instantiation needs no hand-built registry. App: a prefabs.register(document)->PrefabHandle accessor on ApertureSystemContext (systems/prefabs.ts) + spawn.prefab(handle, {transform, overrides, key/name/tags}) on SpawnCommands (reads doc from registry, instantiatePrefab with componentRegistryFromWorld, applySpawnMetadata, returns root), mirroring the gltf replay path; re-exported through systems.ts, this.prefabs on the system base. Proven by test/serialization/prefab-instantiate.test.ts (3) + test/app/prefab-spawn-route.test.ts (2 instances -> meshDraws===2 at distinct positions). NEXT M7-T6 (render-bridge, M, depends none): runtime material parameter mutation via versioned asset re-registration — patchStandardMaterial(prev, patch) (+ unlit/matcap) in render/materials returns a NEW frozen asset; materials.set(handle, patch) on ApertureSystemContext reads assetsRegistry.get(handle), patches, calls markReady(handle, next) (bumps version -> re-prepares GPU material, no re-extraction, no new handle). M7-T4 DONE (fb1dac4b): whole-world scene document save/load on the M7-T3 codec — NEW packages/simulation/src/serialization/scene-document.ts: ApertureSceneDocument { formatVersion, entities:[{id,components}] }. saveScene(world) enumerates active entities (empty-required query), keys each by a stable index:generation id, serializes components, EXCLUDES the two derived components — WorldTransform (resolver-recomputed) + Children (stale-after-reload index). loadScene(world, doc, {registry}) validates formatVersion (unknown -> aperture.scene.unknownFormatVersion diagnostic + instantiates NOTHING), registers every referenced component into the target world FIRST (elics binds component storage to the most recently registering world, so reused module-level components must be re-registered), creates one entity per record + an oldId->newEntity map, deserializes remapping Entity-typed fields through that map, rebuilds the Children index from the remapped authoritative Parent links, re-attaches WorldTransform, runs resolveWorldTransforms (surfacing cycle diagnostics). The registry is an explicit allow-list (a corrupt document only instantiates whitelisted components). ApertureSceneDocument documented as the SceneHandle payload (assets/types.ts). Simulation-only (ECS state, never snapshots/GPU). Proven by test/serialization/scene-roundtrip.test.ts (3 = the 4 Done-when). NEXT M7-T5 (render-bridge, M, depends T3+T1): turn the dead PrefabHandle into a working blueprint — instantiatePrefab(world, document, overrides) clones an ApertureSceneDocument-shaped subtree (loadScene remap), returns the root + applies a root transform override + per-prefab-id field overrides; surface spawn.prefab(handle, {transform, overrides}) on SpawnCommands + a prefabs.register(document)->PrefabHandle accessor on ApertureSystemContext. M7-T3 DONE (54c3728f): generic schema-driven component (de)serialization codec in NEW packages/simulation/src/serialization/ — component-codec.ts serializeEntityComponents reads each field by its EcsType (Vec2/3/4/Color via getVectorView -> number[], Entity via getValue emitted as a stable index:generation token via serializeEntityRef NEVER a raw index, Int/Float/Boolean/String via getValue, Enum via getValue yielding its stable string value since elics stores the value not an index), excludes derived WorldTransform (DERIVED_COMPONENT_IDS), sorts by component id for deterministic docs; deserializeEntityComponents rebuilds via component-registry.ts createComponentRegistry (a component.id->component allow-list), addComponent per record, remapping Entity-typed fields through an injected resolveEntity (unresolved -> null + diagnostic; unregistered id -> diagnostic, never a throw). Worker-safe (no app/render imports); exported from simulation/src/index.ts. Proven by test/serialization/component-codec.test.ts (4 Done-when). NEXT M7-T4 (simulation, M, depends T3+T1): compose the codec into a versioned ApertureSceneDocument { formatVersion, entities:[{id,components}] } via saveScene(world)/loadScene(world,doc) with an oldId->newEntity remap pass for Entity-typed fields + resolveWorldTransforms; realize the dead SceneHandle payload (simulation/src/assets/types.ts:33); unblocks M7-T5 (prefab). M7-T2 DONE (2f1f6b50): hierarchy accessor surfaced on ApertureSystemContext as this.hierarchy — children(ref)/setParent(child, parent|null)/despawnRecursive(ref) in NEW packages/app/src/systems/hierarchy.ts (createHierarchyAccess), resolving EcsEntityRef->Entity through the shared generation-checked resolveActiveEntity EXTRACTED to packages/app/src/entities/lookup/resolve.ts (barrel-free so the accessor reuses it without a context import cycle; mutation.ts now consumes it too). setParent wraps the M7-T1 world-preserving reparent and maps cycle/missing-/invalid-world-transform -> structured aperture.hierarchy.* lookup diagnostics. createApertureEntityHierarchy (entities/lookup/hierarchy.ts) now PREFERS the Children index when any active entity carries it: assembles the tree from LocalTransform participants via the authoritative index (O(subtree)) with a Parent-bucketing fallback for no-Children subtrees (glTF replay) — no full ALL-entities scan; the legacy collectActiveEntities full-scan remains the fallback for no-Children worlds. Accessor + Children re-exported through systems.ts; this.hierarchy exposed on the system base. All 3 Done-when via test/app/hierarchy-accessor.test.ts (this.hierarchy.setParent preserves the child world transform + updates the published parent ref; despawnRecursive drops the next snapshot's meshDraws by the subtree mesh count; Children path == legacy tree WITHOUT calling collectActiveEntities via vi.spyOn) + cycle/stale-ref diagnostics. M7-T1 DONE (c1509d62): bidirectional transform hierarchy — packages/simulation/src/transform/hierarchy.ts setParent (world-preserving reparent, Bevy reparented_to) + getChildren + despawnRecursive + cycle rejection; NEW derived Children component (JSON index:generation refs) registered in registerTransformComponents, Parent stays authoritative; proven by test/transform/hierarchy.test.ts. NEXT M7-T3 (simulation, M, depends none): generic headless-safe component (de)serialization codec in packages/simulation/src/serialization/ — schema-driven serializeEntityComponents/deserializeEntityComponents via a component.id->component registry, Entity-typed fields as stable index:generation tokens, WorldTransform excluded (derived); unblocks M7-T4 (scene save/load) + M7-T5 (prefab). M3 (render graph) COMPLETE 7/7 prior (commits 7d890b57/9acbfdb2/3349eb9a/2a12985b/2f3474b8 — ships behind useFrameGraph; CI green: workspace check + SwiftShader E2E). M1/M2/M4/M5 also complete. ENV: WebGPU E2E runs here on headed Chrome/Metal (real GPU) — SwiftShader+xvfb unavailable (xvfb could not install); cloud CI runs SwiftShader+xvfb via scripts/webgpu-e2e.sh; dof.spec.ts times out under SwiftShader (pre-existing).
